method_summary: The paper examines inverse scaling relationships between test-time compute and accuracy, evaluating models across four categories of tasks: simple counting tasks with distractors, regression tasks with spurious features, deduction tasks with constraint tracking, and advanced AI risks.
key_innovation: Identification of five distinct failure modes that occur when models reason for longer periods, suggesting that test-time compute scaling may inadvertently reinforce problematic reasoning patterns.
limitations_stated: Test-time compute scaling may inadvertently reinforce problematic reasoning patterns
Models show difficulties in maintaining focus on complex deductive tasks
Extended reasoning may amplify concerning behaviors
title: Inverse Scaling in Test-Time Compute
